pages.title: Biome Rule Basics
pages.text: Biome rules apply to specific biomes or biome tags. They're $(bold)medium priority$()-structure rules override them.$(br2)File location:$(br2)$odata/<namespace>/mob_levels/biomes/<name>.json$

pages.title: Targeting Fields
pages.text: Use $(bold)biome$() OR $(bold)biome_tags$() (or both):$(br2)$(li)$(o)biome$(): Single biome ID$(br2)  Example: $(o)"biome": "minecraft:deep_dark"$()$(br2)$(li)$(o)biome_tags$(): List of tag IDs$(br2)  Example: $(o)"biome_tags": ["minecraft:is_nether"]$()$(br2)Match is OR-either condition satisfies.

pages.title: Optional Fields
pages.text: $(bold)priority$() (int, default: 50)$(br2)$(bold)enabled$() (boolean, default: true)$(br2)$(bold)level_mode$() (string, default: distance)$(br2)$(bold)ignore_distance_scaling$() (boolean, default: false)$(br2)$(bold)distance_multiplier$() (double, default: 1.0)$(br2)$(bold)level_range$() (object, default: 1-100)$(br2)$(bold)attribute_scaling$()$(br2)$(bold)mob_overrides$()

pages.title: Biome Tag Priority
pages.text: When multiple biome-tag rules could match:$(br2)$(li)Rules are sorted by $(bold)descending priority$()$(li)First match wins$(br2)Direct biome rules override tag rules of equal priority.$(br2)Example: A rule for $(o)minecraft:deep_dark$() beats a rule for $(o)minecraft:is_overworld$() at the same priority.
